summary: Two Tcl integration tests failed during execution due to Unicode surrogate pair handling. The tests expected properly encoded emoji characters but received raw UTF-16 surrogate pairs from Tcl's eval() function, causing assertion failures.
root_cause: Unicode encoding regression in Tcl/Python integration. Tests test_eval_surrogates_in_result and test_evalfile_surrogates_in_result failed because tcl.eval() returned raw UTF-16 surrogate pairs ('<\ud83d\udcbb>') instead of the expected properly encoded emoji character ('<\U0001f4bb>').

evidence.reason: Direct assertion comparison failure showing actual surrogate pair vs expected emoji representation
Explicit test assertion that expects Tcl to return properly encoded emoji character
Confirms test_tcl module execution resulted in 2 assertion failures
Indicates consistent failure across re-run, ruling out transient flakiness

suggested_fix: Investigate the Unicode surrogate pair handling in the Tcl integration layer, specifically how Tcl eval() results are converted from UTF-16 surrogate pairs to proper Unicode characters. Review recent changes to the Tcl bindings or bytecode generation that may have affected string encoding/decoding.
